steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The blank separates an independent clause on the left (“light intensity affects the chemical reaction rate of photosynthesis”) from a dependent clause on the right (“as light intensity increases”) followed by an independent clause (“so does the rate...”).

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
A comma is required before a dependent clause that introduces a correlative construction, to separate the main clause from the parallel structure. Option B correctly provides this comma: “rate of photosynthesis, as light intensity increases, so does the rate...”

Option A (and) uses a coordinating conjunction where it doesn't apply; Option C (colon) is for lists or elaborations, not parallel clauses; Option D (no punctuation) omits the required boundary marker.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
